markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Coordinate parallel indexing tasks with leases, backpressure, cancellation, and orphan reclaim. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: ParallelIndexingLeaseQueue leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ParallelIndexingLeaseQueue is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: Parallel swarm state recovery hardening for MT-209..216

evidence.summary: MT-209..216 implement lane/capability model, claim/lease receipts, mailbox/handoff/checkpoint/recovery atomicity, recovery/reclaim behavior, attribution scrub, and EventLedger-backed state recovery surfaces.

commands.result: PASS; 16/16 parallel swarm state recovery tests passed
PASS; test target registered without duplicate names

evidence.summary: Reviewer passed MT-209..216 after capability, lease, mailbox, receipt, handoff/checkpoint/recovery, and claim/release atomicity hardening.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-216 requires a Postgres-native ParallelIndexingLeaseQueue coordinating parallel indexing with leases, backpressure, cancellation, and orphan reclaim under PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The committed code (migration 0311/0313 + state_recovery.rs) implements exactly this with a real per-scope serial lease queue, priority-ordered backpressure, TTL orphan reclaim, EventLedger receipts, and quiet-policy enforcement, all proven against the real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL cluster including a 4-thread concurrency race test.
findings: Real lease queue table: migrations/0311_parallel_swarm_state_recovery.sql:115-145 creates knowledge_parallel_indexing_lease_queue with priority, ttl_seconds (CHECK >0), status enum (queued/acquired/completed/cancelled/reclaimed), blocked_by_lease_id, and EventLedger FK. A partial UNIQUE index ux_parallel_indexing_lease_queue_active_scope (scope_kind, scope_id) WHERE status='acquired' enforces serialization (one writer per scope) at the DB level.

enqueue_indexing_lease (state_recovery.rs:3065-3112) implements real backpressure: if an active or queued writer exists for the scope, the new lease is inserted as Queued with blocked_by_lease_id set to the predecessor; otherwise Acquired. Unique-violation race is caught and retried as Queued. try_acquire_indexing_lease (3114-3148) and the _tx variants give a non-blocking path.

acquire_next_indexing_lease (3393-3465) promotes the highest-priority queued lease (ORDER BY priority DESC, enqueued_at_utc ASC) to acquired in one transaction, recomputes expiry from ttl_seconds, and attaches a fresh KNOWLEDGE_INDEX_RUN_STARTED EventLedger receipt. reclaim_orphaned_indexing_leases (3467-3526) reclaims expired acquired leases (expires_at_utc <= NOW()) under FOR UPDATE, setting status=reclaimed and writing a KNOWLEDGE_INDEX_RUN_CANCELLED receipt.

Capability + attribution gating: enqueue/acquire call require_capability(WriteLocalIndex) and lane.scrubbed_for_persistence() before persisting; cloud lanes are denied (test cloud_lane_is_denied_worktree_claim_and_local_index_write_lease at tests:1011). No SQLite anywhere in the impl (grep clean).

Real-PG proof: parallel_indexing_lease_queue_serializes_same_scope_writers_and_reclaims_orphans (tests:5387-5510) asserts first=Acquired, second=Queued+blocked_by=first, SESSION_QUEUED ledger event, promotion to Acquired with fresh receipt after completion, TTL-forced orphan reclaim with KNOWLEDGE_INDEX_RUN_CANCELLED receipt, and quiet-policy payload. concurrent_same_scope_indexing_lease_records_only_real_outcome_events (tests:5512-5616, 4 worker threads, barrier + injected event delay) asserts exactly one Acquired / one Queued under contention. recovery_store()/knowledge_pg() (knowledge_pg_support.rs:108) run all migrations on the real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL cluster in an isolated schema; harness SKIPs loudly only when PG binaries are absent.

Sub-rule 1: grep of state_recovery.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/pending MT-/stub/mock/placeholder returned no hits on the lease path. Sub-rule 3: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not self-signed COMPLETED) - clean handoff.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): packet/MT spec_anchors cite bundle v02.192; current is v02.194. Expected during governance wind-down and not a failing reason. quiet_policy_jsonb column referenced by the insert is added by migration 0313, not 0311 - both are in the same migration chain and tests pass, so no behavioral gap.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
